utterance: find documentation on the open web for Rust async traits

assertions.text: the agent should read skills/web-search/SKILL.md before searching; the skill defines the 5-movement loop and source hierarchy
the agent should apply the 5-movement loop (frame -> reformulate -> inspect -> cross-reference -> stop/surface), not a single-pass search
the response should prefer primary sources (docs.rs, Rust RFCs, the Rust reference) over secondary aggregators (StackOverflow, blogs)
the response should cite ≥2 specific URLs (with stable identifiers like docs.rs/std/...) for the Rust async traits documentation

the response should note the recency of sources (e.g. async-trait crate status, whether Rust 1.75+ stabilized native async traits) and surface limitations of the search (e.g. 'official std docs are sparse on this topic')

reference_solution: Agent reads web-search/SKILL.md, applies 5-movement loop on 'Rust async traits', cites docs.rs/async-trait, Rust RFC 3185 (stabilization), and a recent blog post; notes that native async fn in traits is stable since 1.75.
